text.title: Unit Testing Rules
text.summary: Concrete rules for test structure, naming, isolation, and mock usage.

rule.statement: Structure every test as Arrange / Act / Assert with blank lines between phases.
rule.rationale: The three-phase structure makes the intent of each test scannable and prevents interleaved setup-and-assertion patterns that obscure failures.

rule.statement: Name tests as test_<behavior>_when_<context> or test_should_<expected>_when_<condition>.
rule.rationale: The name must be readable as a failure message. test_calculate_total_returns_zero_for_zero_quantity conveys more than test_calculate_total.

rule.statement: Use one test per behavior, not one test per method.
rule.rationale: Methods have multiple behaviors (normal path, error path, edge case). Grouping by method makes suites non-exhaustive and failure messages ambiguous.

rule.statement: Each test must be independent: no shared mutable state, no execution-order dependencies.
rule.rationale: Shared mutable state causes intermittent failures that depend on test execution order — the hardest category of bug to diagnose.

rule.statement: Mock only at the process boundary (network, disk, external clock). Use fakes or in-memory implementations for internal collaborators.
rule.rationale: MagicMock accepts any method call, including ones that do not exist on the real object. Fakes exercise real contracts. Mocking too deep tests the mock, not the behavior.

rule.statement: Use spec= or autospec=True when a unittest.mock.Mock is unavoidable.
rule.rationale: Without spec, mock.foo.bar.baz() returns a Mock silently. spec= constrains the mock to the interface of the real object.

rule.statement: Prefer pytest fixtures over setUp methods: fixtures are composable, scope-aware (function / module / session), and parametrizable.
rule.rationale: setUp runs before every test in the class regardless of need. Fixtures compose, can be shared across modules, and support teardown via yield.

rule.statement: Pin time and randomness in tests: use freezegun or time-machine for datetime.now(), and random.seed(0) for pseudo-random values.
rule.rationale: Tests that fail twice a year at midnight UTC represent the worst class of flake: rare, environment-dependent, and hardest to reproduce locally.

rule.statement: Keep the unit test suite under 30 seconds total. If slower, the suite includes integration tests; mark and separate them.
rule.rationale: A suite that takes minutes stops being run on every save. Speed is the primary property that makes unit tests useful as a feedback loop.
